title: slack send/reply: per-CWD reply context prevents cross-project thread misroute
desc: SaveReplyContext stored channel+threadTs in a single hq-wide file;  from project A therefore replayed the threadTs last saved by project B, misrouting briefings across projects. Fixed: filename now keyed by SHA256-hash-8 of caller CWD AND the plaintext CWD is stored+verified on load. Stale cross-project contexts are refused with an explicit log line.
steps: slack send saves context at DataDir/slack_reply_ctx.<cwd-hash>.txt with cwd/channel/thread fields
slack reply loads from the same per-cwd filename
If stored cwd in the file mismatches caller cwd (legacy shared file), load returns null with a warn log and reply refuses to post
Hash uses SHA256 of lowercased+trim-slash cwd so path casing doesn't split contexts
Effect: auto-briefings from DG-WKAppBot and DG-WkAutoQuant never overwrite each other's thread context
author: claude
